vocabulary.name: Celcoin API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Key terms, tags, and concepts extracted from Celcoin OpenAPI specifications

domainTerms.definition: Brazil's instant payment system operated by the Central Bank of Brazil (BCB), enabling 24/7 real-time transfers
Brazilian payment slip used for bill payment and cash collection, widely used in e-commerce and B2B payments
Transferência Eletrônica Disponível — Brazilian bank wire transfer, typically same-day during business hours
Cédula de Crédito Bancário — Brazilian bank credit note instrument for loan formalization
Banking as a Service — embedded financial infrastructure enabling non-bank companies to offer banking products
Iniciador de Transação de Pagamento — Payment Transaction Initiator under Brazil's Open Finance framework
Know Your Customer — identity verification and onboarding processes required for financial account opening
Fast Identity Online — biometric authentication standard used in Celcoin's account linking for Open Finance
Centro Nacional de Automação Bancária — Brazilian banking automation standard for batch payment file processing
Consigned credit — payroll-deducted loan product, automatically deducted from salary or benefits
Cédula de Crédito ao Agronegócio or Comercial Aceleradora — commercial credit note for financial operations
Brazil's open banking/finance initiative mandated by BCB, enabling data sharing and payment initiation across institutions
Baixa de boleto — the settlement/cancellation process for boletos (payment slips) after payment is received
Pessoa Física — individual (human) person in Brazilian financial/legal context
Pessoa Jurídica — corporate/business entity in Brazilian financial/legal context
Conta escrow — a controlled financial account holding funds on behalf of a third party during a transaction
Proposta — credit or account onboarding proposal submitted for approval in the Celcoin platform
Saldo devedor — the remaining balance owed on a credit instrument such as CCB
